title: Carphalea pubescens
taxonomy: Carphalea pubescens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Carphalea, which is a group of plants native to Madagascar. The species Carphalea pubescens is further classified as a shrub or small tree. It is native to the island of Madagascar and is often found in dry forests and scrublands. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and distribution.

intro: Carphalea pubescens, also known as the velvet rockrose, is a flowering plant species native to Madagascar. The health benefits of Carphalea pubescens include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments, including skin issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Carphalea pubescens is often prepared as a tea or infusion, although it can also be found in capsule or tincture form, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be utilized for potential therapeutic benefits.
